issue_title: AWS provisioning process fails in 1.9.0
issue_body:
`gen-json.py` in the AWS provisioning process adds in some AWS-specific user-data and also modifies portions of the user-data for AWS.  In AWS, the CloudFormation templates we use specify launch configuration for CoreOS hosts wherein we mount a few EBS volumes-- among them, we mount one to `/media/etcd`.  One of the corresponding modification to the user-data is to string replace certain instances of `/var/lib/etcd2` with `/media/etcd`

See relevant lines from `gen-json.py` here:

```
# configure etcd to use its EBS volume
dump = dump.replace('-data-dir /var/lib/etcd2', '-data-dir /media/etcd')
dump = dump.replace('--volume=/var/lib/etcd2', '--volume=/media/etcd')
```

https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/contrib/aws/gen-json.py#L108-L109

This worked great until v1.9.0 when one of the strings that is ordinarily modified was changed in `user-data.example`.

Here's the relevant commit:

https://github.com/deis/deis/commit/ccb2f9c8ce4dc4d2589bae7ea8f3bc7e87bb315d

What this results in is user-data that's actually used that looks something like this:

```
  - name: etcd.service
    # ...
      [Service]
      # ...
      Environment="ETCD_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/etcd2"
      # ...
      ExecStart=/usr/bin/docker run --net=host --rm \
        --volume=${ETCD_DATA_DIR}:/var/lib/etcd2 \
        # ...
        -data-dir /media/etcd \
        # ...
    # ...
```

As you can see, the wrong directory on the host (should be `/media/etcd`, but is `/var/lib/etcd2`) is mounted into the wrong directory on the container (should be `/media/etcd`, but is `/var/lib/etcd2`).  The containerized etcd2, however, per `-data-dir /media/etcd` is looking for the host's `/media/etcd' to have been mounted into the container's `/media/etcd`... which isn't the case.

This _is_ definitely wrong.  The very odd part about it however, is that whatever issue this actually causes appears to be non-deterministic.  Sometimes this fails spectacularly; sometimes it _somehow_ seems to work (although I'm not sure how).

issue_title: k8s: Use Downward API to expose namespace, pod name
issue_body:
It is probably a good practice for us to use the Kubernetes Downward API to add some environment variables into the container. Some simple changes to the pod spec in the rc would get us some useful information.

Downward API:
http://kubernetes.io/v1.0/docs/user-guide/downward-api/
Example: 
https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/kubernetes/blob/master/docs/user-guide/downward-api/dapi-pod.yaml

Things I think we should probably expose:

- APP_NAME (pod name)
- APP_NAMESPACE (pod namespace)
- APP_LABELS (if we can get these into a pretty list)

Are there other pieces of metadata that might be good to add? Is there a way we can get the Docker image and label names? That would be useful for debugging.

If we're going to quickly switch the app name to be the same as DEIS_APP, then APP_NAME is probably redundant.

issue_title: Control python warnings with an env var
issue_body:
I think this might just be something that we hook into `GLOBUS_CLI_DEBUGMODE`.
We should suppress warnings during normal operations -- they're chatty and confusing for end users.
However, being able to see warnings is a useful feature for developers working on the project, so being able to expose them seems good.

issue_title: Return rows as native Python lists
issue_body:
Currently, turbodbc returns rows as a dedicated type created by boost::python. This type is not directly printable and adds confusion. Replace this type with a standard Python list.

In addition, before turbodbc converts result set values to Python objects, there is an intermediate step in converting result set values to a C++ `std::vector<turbodbc::optional_field>`, which is in turn converted to a Python object. This is pretty costly and unnecessary. Both issues can be fixed in a single effort.

issue_title: Duplicate environments in conda env list
issue_body:
<!--
Hi!  Read this; it's important.

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a bug report or feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:
      ==> file at the corresponding feedstock under https://github.com/conda-forge
  * repo.anaconda.com access and service:
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

If you continue on, and especially if you are submitting a bug report,

   **please include all requested information below.**

If a maintainer determines the information is required to understand 
your issue, and if it is not provided, your issue may be
closed automatically.

-->

**I'm submitting a...**
  - [x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request

## Current Behavior

In environment which's paths are not case sensitive, like Windows. Sometimes there may be duplicate environment prefixes in `~/.conda/environments.txt`.

I get case like this:

    > conda env list
    # conda environments:
    #
    base                  *  C:\Miniconda3
                             c:\Miniconda3
    
    # notice the case differences

I can resolve this with manually fixing the `environments.txt`. However I think it's best to prevent it happening from beginning.

issue_title: mark a specific package from a specific channel as bad or mark a package to always come from a specific channel
issue_body:
In some cases it could be good to not get a package from a specific channel (e.g. when conda-forge brings out a new matplotlib and the default packages has a faulty one or so...). See https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/22 for the disccusion.


There could be two ways to implment that:
* marking packages as bad ("channel/package version") -> everything which matches that is ignored
* markign packages as good: ("channel/package") -> every package with that name from a different channel gets ignored. 

An idea for the "mark bad" would be this:

* add a new method in `Resolve` ([code](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/19c2d2e2393d6dd89cb8f051858da27f5cc8a78f/conda/resolve.py#L187) to remove all packages from the index, which have a specific name, version and channel 
* add a file named `<env-prefix>/conda-meta/marked-bad` which lists `channel/package version`, and then in https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/05366e9a259e0eb5301f0cb822781fadac100cad/conda/plan.py#L402 interate over this file and remove items by calling the new method on `r`

Alternative:
* add the removal directly to `get_index` if there is a `prefix/conda-meta/marked-bad` [code](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/d3a76d3a0afbd31358464216a423806bb8e97e4f/conda/api.py#L44) 

Implementing the opposite would be almost the same, only you would give a spec ("channel/package") and then all packages with that name but not from the channel (`channel not in info["channel"]`) would be removed.

issue_title: `get_cigar_stats` documentation is inaccurate since support for B-tag was added
issue_body:
The `AlignedSegment.get_cigar_stats` method returns arrays of length 11, not length 10 as implied by the docstring.

The discrepancy is due to the addition of support for a "B" cigar tag that has not been reflected in the docstring (see e6c9bda, although the release notes suggest that the B tag has been recognised since pysam version 0.7).

The `AlignedSegment.get_cigar_stats` is as follows:

```
        """summary of operations in cigar string.
        The output order in the array is "MIDNSHP=X" followed by a
        field for the NM tag. If the NM tag is not present, this
        field will always be 0.
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |M    |BAM_CMATCH    |0    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |I    |BAM_CINS      |1    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |D    |BAM_CDEL      |2    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |N    |BAM_CREF_SKIP |3    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |S    |BAM_CSOFT_CLIP|4    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |H    |BAM_CHARD_CLIP|5    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |P    |BAM_CPAD      |6    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |=    |BAM_CEQUAL    |7    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |X    |BAM_CDIFF     |8    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |NM   |NM tag        |9    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        If no cigar string is present, empty arrays will be returned.
        Parameters
        ----------
        Returns
        -------
        arrays : two arrays. The first contains the nucleotide counts within
           each cigar operation, the second contains the number of blocks for
           each cigar operation.
        """
```

I propose this be ammended to something like:

```
        """summary of operations in cigar string.
        The output order in the array is "MIDNSHP=XB" followed by a
        field for the NM tag. If the NM tag is not present, this
        field will always be 0.
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |M    |BAM_CMATCH    |0    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |I    |BAM_CINS      |1    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |D    |BAM_CDEL      |2    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |N    |BAM_CREF_SKIP |3    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |S    |BAM_CSOFT_CLIP|4    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |H    |BAM_CHARD_CLIP|5    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |P    |BAM_CPAD      |6    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |=    |BAM_CEQUAL    |7    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |X    |BAM_CDIFF     |8    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |B    |B tag         |9    |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        |NM   |NM tag        |10   |
        +-----+--------------+-----+
        If no cigar string is present, empty arrays will be returned.
        Parameters
        ----------
        Returns
        -------
        arrays : two arrays. The first contains the nucleotide counts within
           each cigar operation, the second contains the number of blocks for
           each cigar operation.
        """
```

The B tag is not standard SAM and I haven't been able to find a definition: can anyone shed light on what it means? Someone who knows what this B tag is about might suggest a better entry for the table.

issue_title: END coordinates in VCF files
issue_body:
I'm trying to parse VCF files with perhaps incomplete headers. This generally works okay, but I'm not sure how best to parse the "END" coordinate if it's not defined in the header. It seems to only be parsed into `variant.stop` when "END" been defined in the header -- which would be okay, except I also can't get it out of `variant.info` since pysam insists that "END" is a reserved keyword (https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/blob/master/pysam/libcbcf.pyx#L2525). I'm not sure how much of this behavior is caused by pysam or by htslib, but ideally `variant.stop` would get set irrespective of the header value. I can get around this with this awkward workaround:

```
if not "END" in vcf.header.info:
    vcf.header.add_line(
        """##INFO=<ID=END,Number=1,Type=Integer,Description="End coordinate">"""
```

See also #402 -- I regularly see VCF files without any headers at all and it'd be great if pysam could deal more nicely with this case.

issue_title: Exclude should only be True when explicitely set to True
issue_body:
In a curator action file you can set `exclude: True` on a filter to negate its effect. As setting this accidentally can destroy much data, this setting should only accept "True" or "False" or empty (meaning the default: False). However, adding a yaml list below exclude is also interpreted as True.

Here is an example:

Intended configuration that works as expected, removing all indices starting with test, older than 14 days:

```yaml
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Delete indices older than 14 days
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: test
        exclude:
      - filtertype: age
        source: name
        direction: older
        timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
        unit: days
        unit_count: 14
```

Accidentally wrongly indented configuration that deletes all indices except those starting with test:

```yaml
actions:
  1:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Delete indices older than 14 days
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: test
        exclude:
        - filtertype: age
          source: name
          direction: older
          timestring: '%Y.%m.%d'
          unit: days
          unit_count: 14
```

Tested on:
Curator Version: 4.2.6
Elasticsearch Version: 5.2.2

issue_title: Enhance indices.exists_alias() behaviour
issue_body:
Currently if you call `indices.exists_alias(index=indexname)`  without giving any value for the parameter `name`, it will return `False` even if the index has one or several aliases. According to the HTTP API this behaviour is wrong, such a call should return `True`. 
That could be simply corrected either by adding a condition evaluating the name before making the request or by setting the default value of name at an empty string.

issue_title: CLI Component Story
issue_body:
### Description

Native package manager installs (MSI, Docker image, apt-get, Homebrew) don't support the CLI 'az component update/add' story as:
- Self-updating packages are frowned upon with many of the above package managers.
- Similar story with downloading and installing new code from untrusted stores.

Suggestions:
(1) Remove 'az component update', 'az component remove' & 'az component list-available'.
    The remaining command will be 'az component list'.
    Rationale:
        * These commands aren't supported in most install methods any way (see above).
        * We include all modules from our repo in our default install anyway.

(2) Update docs.microsoft.com install page.
    Remove messages like 'this install does not support components'.
    Rationale:
        * If we're going to remove the above commands, we should remove doc references to it.

(3) What if I have a special use-case where I want extension or preview modules optionally installed?
    Separate instructions can be provided in these scenarios.
        - Create a Python virtual environment (`virtualenv myenv`)
        - Provide a `requirements.txt` file that instructs pip on the required packages. (This could point to your private GitHub repo, storage account or other location).
        - Install with pip `pip install -r <path_to_requirements.txt>`

(4) In the future, if we need the ability to hide/show commands we can look at that when it becomes an issue.

Affects: `pip` and `interactive script` installs mostly.

cc: @mayurid, @johanste, @JasonRShaver

issue_title: Issues with 'az network dns record-set list'
issue_body:
Issues with 'az network dns record-set list'
- [x] 1. As with 'create' and 'delete', should support -z as short form for --zone-name (PR #1129)
- [ ] 2a. <del>No way to format results as table whilst still showing record values (RDATA). </del> _This will require a change to our output infrastructure and is being tracked by item #1948_
- [x] 2b. Also, the record type is not shown. This is a serious regression relative to the old CLI and makes the list output useless. (PR #1823).
- [x] 3. Doesn't show metadata in table format either (PR #1823)
- [x] 4. JSON output should not show 'null' output for each record type that doesn't apply to the record being listed.  Instead, these lines should be suppressed. (PR #1592)
- [x] 5. Doesn't support --type for listing record sets of a specific type only.  This is a regression vs old CLI. (PR #1823)

issue_title: Token expirations are not checked on `decode`
issue_body:
https://github.com/ONSdigital/sdc-cryptography/blob/53c151424accfa5a7474a81d1afeddf78ea154cf/sdc/crypto/jwt_helper.py#L17

This line sets the default argument of `check_claims` to be `{}`. As a result, jwcrypto does not call the `_check_exp` method: https://github.com/latchset/jwcrypto/blob/master/jwcrypto/jwt.py#L343

Ideally the default `check_claims` argument would be `None`.

This would potentially be a breaking change.

issue_title: Install node packages from npm instead of git
issue_body: The node ecosystem is very much centered around npm, and therefore it would make sense to install hooks from there instead of grabbing them from git repositories. This is particularly true for packages which perform non-trivial work when creating a release, like compiling native binaries, minifying resources, cross-compiling various languages, including git submodules and whatnot. The node package will usually contain all of this, the source tree likely not. I guess the same might be true for Python with PyPI, and other common repositories for other languages.

I am using pybullet to compute distances between a bunch of objects in a scene, but my RAM gets full very quickly... I tried to profile using `memory_profiler` and it seems that the memory allocated by `getClosestPoints` is never freed.

```python
import pybullet_data
import os

nr_steps = 1000

for i in range(nr_steps):

p.disconnect()
```

issue_title: Force keys to str instances
issue_body:
Right now a key type is dark corner: it could be str, istr or something other.
For sake of further optimizations it should be `str` always.

Passing `istr` as key to any miltidict API is still allowed and encouraged: it saves execution time by getting rid of `s.title()` conversion.

But `istr` type is ambiguous by it nature: we can define equality for `str` and `istr` by comparing their *canonical* representations but ordering is cumbersome.
Ordering by canonical reprs leads to very tricky errors, the same for *original* reprs.
Also CPython is very optimized for `str` but not classes derived from it.

I believe the change doesn't touch our users but aiohttp might get speedup on implementing the issue.

issue_title: Jupyter notebook key-binding management is very cumbersome
issue_body:
I'll preface this by saying that jupyter notebooks are awesome and I'm extremely grateful to you guys for creating this tool. I use it all the time for my research.

I often mess with the mappings in my `.vimrc` while writing code with VIM, change my System Preferences key bindings on my Mac, and change my iTerm2 key bindings. So I'm used to having total, autocratic control over my key-bindings for the tools I use. But so far this seems very difficult to do cleanly with jupyter notebooks.

* When using the help-menu GUI to change mappings, the results appear to be **local to the current *session* of the current notebook**. Re-freshing the notebook (even after saving it) deletes these changes, and the changes are not synced across other existing notebooks.

* I see the actual bindings are stored in `.jupyter/nbconfig/notebook.json`. However if I directly edit this file, there is no way to have my edits reflected in currently existing notebooks -- only completely new notebooks will reflect these changes.

* Another quirk is that, when using the GUI to change mappings, they are permanently added to the global `notebook.json` file (and new mappings of the same key will overwrite old mappings) -- despite the fact that the mappings are reset to the defaults if the notebook is reloaded!

Let me know if you can't reproduce some of these issues. My jupyter version is 4.2.1.

Would love to see in a future version some more clean controls for jupyter notebook key-bindings -- perhaps a page on the official documentation about changing `~/.jupyter/nbconfig/notebook.json`, and having notebooks automatically scan this file just like they seem to automatically scan the `~/.jupyter/custom/custom.css` and `~/.jupyter/custom/custom.js` files.

issue_title: API Authorization type is basic - should probably be bearer
issue_body:
The API expects `Authorization: basic <api password>`. The basic authentication method requires a username and password with a specific encoding of a username and password pair. 

Would the `bearer` type would be more appropriate?

See: https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/6c840c0a170bf8fc7686381e2ed9b7edbe487008/blockstack_client/rpc.py#L417

issue_title: Incorrect F401/F822 results when `__all__` is defined from concatenated lists
issue_body:
Using Python 2.7.13 & pyflakes 1.5.0, and this testcase (extracted from [this file from isort](https://github.com/timothycrosley/isort/blob/0aca83884667f1b20605e3bbef48bacf4f8b77fa/isort/pie_slice.py)):
```python
import sys

PY2 = sys.version_info[0] == 2
PY3 = sys.version_info[0] == 3

common_exports = ['PY2', 'PY3', 'this_does_not_exist']

if PY2:
    from itertools import ifilter as filter
    __all__ = common_exports + ['filter', 'this_does_not_exist_either']
else:
    __all__ = common_exports
```

**Expected:**
* `F822 undefined name 'this_does_not_exist' in __all__`
* `F822 undefined name 'this_does_not_exist_either' in __all__`

**Actual:**
* No F822 errors displayed (when there should be two).
* A false positive `F401 'itertools.ifilter as filter' imported but unused`.

It appears that flake8 ignores the `__all__` export entirely if it is defined from concatenated lists. ie:
```python 
__all__ = [] + ['this_will_be_ignored']

__all__ = ['so_will_this'] + []
```

````python
issue_title: Spaces etc in enums not escaped in RDF
issue_body:
As pointed out in common-workflow-language/cwltool#444 using enum symbols with spaces will cause problems with generating RDF, as the spaces are not escaped.

There are also problems with generating the JSON-LD Context if the string after the space is repeated, as it tries to insert for instance `table` multiple into `@context`:

> Exception: Predicate collision on table, 'file:///c:/users/stain/src/schema_salad/schema_salad/tests/../tests/test_schema/symbols-with-spaces.yml#table_type/OTU table' != 'file:///c:/users/stain/src/schema_salad/schema_salad/tests/../tests/test_schema/symbols-with-spaces.yml#table_type/Pathway table

It is currently not defined anywhere what characters are allowed in valid schema salad identifiers - meaning anything is currently allowed, including newline, `"` and anything that can be expressed in YAML escapes.

A real fix is to restrict the characters of all short-hand identifiers, e.g. to be valid [IRI](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3987.txt) fragment identifiers.  

Some workflows already started using this, like in https://github.com/ProteinsWebTeam/ebi-metagenomics-cwl/blob/e14f5cd5e240ecb2563d138e9aa112abcdae3295/tools/biom-convert-table.yaml where constants like `Pathway table` (with space) is passed in as argument.

A quick fix, as in 8514cb72996175aac8f749d57655dde835f0d195, will seem sufficient, but does not add required 'unquoting'. To be able to unquote we need to both have a predictable quote pattern and know all the places that needs the "pure" values. For instance the above commit breaks that workflow, as it changes the constant to `Pathway_table` which the command line tool would not understand.

See the https://github.com/common-workflow-language/schema_salad/tree/escape-enums branch for my naive approach (do not merge!).

issue_body:
The geo_json() method only provides fixed number of color maps and upto six threshold values.
This imposes severe limitation when a custom color mapping is desired. Conceivably one could pass pass a custom color table or lookup table as a tuple for example. 

Perhaps add an argument colormap = list of value, red, green, blue, opacity tuples
An option to interpolate colors will be awesome, as currently that is missing as well.

```
# Lookup table with (Value, Red, Green, Blue) tuples 
lookup = [ (-0.5, 255, 0 , 0),
                 (0.0, 0, 255 , 0),
                 (0.5, 0, 0 , 255),
               ]
````

Even better would an ability to also provide transparency, although this may require too much work
```
# Lookup table with (Value, Red, Green, Blue, Opacity) tuples 
lookup = [ (-0.5, 255, 0 , 0, 128),
                 (0.0, 0, 255 , 0, 255),
                 (0.5, 0, 0 , 255, 192),
               ]
```

issue_title: sync compatibility with b2sync command
issue_body:
Backblaze has a sync tool written in C.  It would be nice if the sync command in Python were compatible.

I think the only issue is that the C tool has an option for encoding the modification time in the file name.  This makes scanning for updates faster because it doesn't have to call `b2_get_file_info` on each file.

issue_title: Order of pre-processing and reading data into dict
issue_body:
Currently, preprocessors are called after the output dictionary is constructed. This makes it impossible to e.g. unwrap the namespacing operation given in the examples purely within a deserializer, as far as I can tell.

It's also surprising that the preprocessor runs before output dictionary construction, because this makes it not parallel postprocessors on `Schema.dump`.

- [x] `func` parameter of `fields.Function`

issue_body:
I created a simple python script that demonstrates the problem. It is here: https://github.com/lauriebyrum/CoreML-Custom-Layers/blob/master/convert_lambda.py

Without this change https://github.com/lauriebyrum/CoreML-Custom-Layers/commit/ba5dc0b878eccd33f495203c203f4224627f871b, the model can be converted to coreml and the model will compile. WIth that change, the converter succeeds, but the compiler rejects it: 
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/coremlc compile NeuralMcNeuralNet.mlmodel .
Input name(s) and shape(s): 
image : (C,H,W) = (3, 256, 256) 
Neural Network compiler 0: 100 , name = conv2d_1, output shape : (C,H,W) = (6, 256, 256) 
Neural Network compiler 1: 500 , name = lambda_1, output shape : (C,H,W) = (-1, -1, -1) 
Neural Network compiler 2: 300 , name = reshape_1, output shape : (C,H,W) = (6, 256, 256) 
Neural Network compiler 3: 120 , name = global_average_pooling2d_1, output shape : (C,H,W) = (6, 1, 1) 
Neural Network compiler 4: 140 , name = dense_1, output shape : (C,H,W) = (10, 1, 1) 
Neural Network compiler 5: 175 , name = dense_1__activation__, output shape : (C,H,W) = (10, 1, 1) 
coremlc: Error: compiler error:  Invalid dst shape-1 x -1 x -1 x -1->-1->256 x 256 x 6 x 0 x 1 x 

I have looked for a way to fix the converter, but have not found one. Help? 

Meanwhile, I'll try rolling the Reshape into the Lambda, but we generally want to allow our developers to develop in keras and not have to change the model to get it into coreml. 

Thanks in advance for any advice.

issue_title: docs/getting-started.md example doesn't work.
issue_body:
Following the [running the example app](https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/master/docs/getting-started.md#running-the-example-app) instructions results in several errors:
- `runtests.py` is missing: not found anywhere in the repo. Did you mean `django-admin.py test`?
- `django-admin.py runserver` errors out with:
```
django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: Requested setting DEBUG, but settings are not configured. You must either define the environment variable DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE or call settings.configure() before accessing settings.
```
Trying to set `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE` complains about other missing stuff:
```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'polymorphic'
```
Here's what `pip freeze` shows:
```
Django==2.0.2
djangorestframework==3.7.7
-e git+https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api.git@c5d34e2ea67bab8a4e9570f54779911570f24c83#egg=djangorestframework_jsonapi
inflection==0.3.1
packaging==16.8
pyparsing==2.2.0
pytz==2017.3
six==1.11.0
```
I attempted these fixes:
```bash
pip install django-polymorphic
pip install django-debug_toolbar
export DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=example.settings.dev
django-admin.py runserver
...
django.core.management.base.SystemCheckError: SystemCheckError: System check identified some issues:

ERRORS:
?: debug_toolbar.middleware.DebugToolbarMiddleware is missing from MIDDLEWARE.
	HINT: Add debug_toolbar.middleware.DebugToolbarMiddleware to MIDDLEWARE.
```
which is weird since:
```bash
grep -nr debug_toolbar.middleware.DebugToolbarMiddleware .
...
./example/settings/dev.py:63:    'debug_toolbar.middleware.DebugToolbarMiddleware',
```
I've successfully used most of DJA based on [usage.md](https://github.com/django-json-api/django-rest-framework-json-api/blob/master/docs/usage.md) but was looking for some examples, especially for resource-related fields and figured the example app would be a good place to start.

Happy to try and submit a PR with improved documentation if you can provide some guidance on what's supposed to be there. Thanks!

issue_title: Implementation of SSDMultiBoxLoss

And it does not take -1 in cls_target into consideration, where -1 often means ignoring the loss.

issue_title: Path Backslash Requirement
issue_body:
Currently, [](https://github.com/oasis-open/cti-python-stix2) accepts 2 backslashes in directory path without interpretation while [](https://github.com/oasis-open/cti-pattern-validator) requires 4 to return no error. 

Not sure if these 2 libraries are expected to be consistent with each other. Currently I work around this by adding **.replace('\\', '\\\\')** to every pattern I am trying to verify.

Here is a sample to demostrate the inconsistency.

```
from stix2 import Indicator, Bundle
from stix2patterns.validator import run_validator

path = "[directory:path LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\%']"

errors = run_validator(path)
#errors = run_validator(path.replace('\\', '\\\\'))

if errors:
	print(errors)
else:
	indicator = Indicator(
					name="Powershell Directory",
					labels=['native tool'],
					pattern=path
				)
	print(str(indicator))

```

issue_title: Clearing cells sets illegal 'outputs' key to markdown cells
issue_body:
This is a little bit related to #1232  and #1233 

Restarting the kernel and clearing cells sets  `outputs` keys to all cells, including markdown cells, where it is invalid.

I'm on master branch commit 974cdd2b05

## Reproducing

1. create of open any notebook with at least one markdown cell
2. use *Language > Restart and Clear All Cells*
3. save the notebook
4. opening the notebook using `jupyter notebook` or `jupyter nbconvert` will fail in the manner below


I discovered this trying to convert a notebook to markdown:

```
jarmo@lambda-epsilon:/tmp$ jupyter nbconvert --to markdown a.ipynb 
[NbConvertApp] Converting notebook a.ipynb to markdown
[NbConvertApp] ERROR | Notebook JSON is invalid: Additional properties are not allowed ('outputs' was unexpected)

Failed validating 'additionalProperties' in markdown_cell:

On instance['cells'][0]:
{'cell_type': 'markdown',
 'metadata': {'collapsed': False, 'outputExpanded': False},
 'outputs': ['...0 outputs...'],
 'source': 'this cell will have an `outputs` key which is invalid'}
[NbConvertApp] Writing 55 bytes to a.md
```

## Fix

Using a text editor to remove all `outputs` keys form cells that have `"cell_type": "markdown"` fixes the errors.

issue_title: Correct resampling of multiple swaths that do not fit into memory
issue_body:
We are currently working on the problem of resampling multiple swaths in a correct manner. Our main concern are edge effects when stitching swaths or parts of orbits together. We mostly use a hamming window for resampling so we do not want to ignore the edges of our swaths. We do have a working solution for the problem and I am currently looking into the best way to open source it.

My main questions are:

1. Would you consider this functionality a part of pyresample or should wrappers around pyresample manage inter-swath resampling.
2. Are you aware of other projects that tackle the same issue which we could contact if your answer to question 1 is no. 

Otherwise we will open source it as our own project and keep you informed if you are interested.

issue_title: Slow submission speed
issue_body:
I have been looking into how long it takes jobs to submit to DIrac.

In total it is about 5 seconds for Ganga, 8 seconds for Dirac on the command line and about 0.1s for the API in a standalone python session.

For us most of the time is taken for the imports of Dirac etc at the start of the `dirac-script.py`, about 4-5s and then dirac.submit(j) is less than a second.

@egede suggested we put several subjobs into one `dirac-script.py`.  Is there anything else we can be doing to speed this up?

issue_title: It is still possible to subvert __setattr__ with some GangaObject inherited classes
issue_body:
This has been a problem which appears to come and go and I've plugged a few areas in the GangaObject and Proxy code where the situation arises.

The problem is basically that a member attribute of a class (which happens to be in the Schema but strictly this shouldn't matter!) fails to have it's _parent attribute assigned upon the attribute being assigned.

I've added some _setParent and #FIXME statements into the Job class (https://github.com/ganga-devs/ganga/commit/9be4d2b58e5a6b0a616e166fd720c29e13084b64) which should be removed and un-needed by the next release.

This 'fix', whilst works, concerns me that this may be broken in more places in the testing or in some usage I'm not currently testing (i.e. maybe the atlas env or similar).


I've debugged this particular problem multiple times before and it often turns out to be some init/construct/(some-other) method which oversteps itself and assigns an item directly subverting the setter methods in the GangaObject.

My plan is to try and trace the setter/getter methods to check for either an assignment which fails to correctly assign a parent to an attribute of GangaObject type and/or to make sure that without a proxy that the bare class always calls the getter/setter methods as intended without the possibility of these methods being ignored for the standard python methods.

issue_title: Suggest using Xenial kernel package for ubuntu 14.04
issue_body:
Currently, the install directions for 14.04 suggest download a specific release of the Ubuntu 15.10 (Wily Werewolf) kernel. The concern here is that locks the user into a specific kernel release which is not being updated and may miss critical security patches.

Instead, suggest using 'apt' and using the Ubuntu 16.04 (Xenial Xerus) kernel:
`sudo apt-get install linux-generic-lts-xenial`
This kernel is currently 4.4.0-18-generic, and should be updated for security fixes.

rs_deproject_pixel_to_point(...)
rs_transform_point_to_point(,...)
rs_project_point_to_pixel(...)

So my question is whether this is a problem with the colour mapping or just an issue with PCL? Sorry, this is more of a forum type question.

issue_title: is_leaf_type() returns True when the type is List of String
issue_body:
```
In [1]: from graphql import GraphQLString, GraphQLList

In [2]: from graphql.type.definition import is_leaf_type

In [3]: is_leaf_type(GraphQLList(GraphQLString))
Out[3]: True
```

See the current `master` implementation [here](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/cfeaa393259266572e634f6e7e8dfd4fc591b2a4/graphql/type/definition.py#L43): `is_leaf_type()` calls `get_named_type()`, which unwinds both lists and non-nulls to get at the inner type. The list unwinding is incorrect in this instance, as a list of strings is clearly a non-leaf type.

This is all using `graphql-core` version `1.0.1`, the newest available on PyPI.

issue_title: Implement Windows Provisioning
SDK consistency
issue_body:
The configuration fields should be consistent between the various SDKs.

Python on Flask uses { "redisHost", "redisPort" }
PHP uses { "cache": { "adapter" .... } }
Python SDK doesn't mention anything.

Python uses "ready"
Ruby uses "block_until_ready"
Node.js uses an event handler
Javascript uses "readyTimeout" + event handler

issue_title: Directory traversal on template_origin variable
issue_body:
We have enabled debug toolbar on our test environment of Django 1.10.17. After auditing the site we have found that template_origin variable is not correctly sanitized. I understand this is a Debug addon that probably isn't aimed for production environments, but I wanted only to report this just as an informative post. 
Maybe this is the intended behavior of this variable. If this is correct, sorry for this post!

Proof of concept:

http://FQDN/__debug__/template_source/?template=pages/home.html&template_origin=/etc/passwd

![screenshot from 2017-08-09 14-36-37](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/7797734/29136999-50ddf02e-7d15-11e7-8f14-916671582187.png)


Affected file: 

panels/templates/views.py

Affected portion of code:

`def template_source(request):
    """
    Return the source of a template, syntax-highlighted by Pygments if
    it's available.
    """
    template_origin_name = request.GET.get('template_origin', None)
    if template_origin_name is None:
        return HttpResponseBadRequest('"template_origin" key is required')
    template_name = request.GET.get('template', template_origin_name)

    final_loaders = []
    loaders = Engine.get_default().template_loaders

    for loader in loaders:
        if loader is not None:
            # When the loader has loaders associated with it,
            # append those loaders to the list. This occurs with
            # django.template.loaders.cached.Loader
            if hasattr(loader, 'loaders'):
                final_loaders += loader.loaders
            else:
                final_loaders.append(loader)

    for loader in final_loaders:
        if Origin:  # django>=1.9
            origin = Origin(template_origin_name)
            try:
                source = loader.get_contents(origin)
                break
            except TemplateDoesNotExist:
                pass
        else:  # django<1.9
            try:
                source, _ = loader.load_template_source(template_name)
                break
            except TemplateDoesNotExist:
                pass
    else:
        source = "Template Does Not Exist: %s" % (template_origin_name,)

    try:
        from pygments import highlight
        from pygments.lexers import HtmlDjangoLexer
        from pygments.formatters import HtmlFormatter

        source = highlight(source, HtmlDjangoLexer(), HtmlFormatter())
        source = mark_safe(source)
        source.pygmentized = True
    except ImportError:
        pass
`
Regards,

```
    from pyexcel_ods import save_data, get_data

    gnsFile = "C:\\users\\main1\\documents\\gns 16-05-16.ods"
```

```
    keys = sheet_dicts.keys()

```

I was able to work around it by changing the body of the write method in pyexcel_io/base.py to the following:

```
        try:
            keys = sheet_dicts.keys()
            for name in keys:
                sheet = self.create_sheet(name)
                if sheet is not None:
                    sheet.write_array(sheet_dicts[name])
                    sheet.close()
        except AttributeError:
            sheet = self.create_sheet('Sheet1')
            if sheet is not None:
                sheet.write_array(sheet_dicts)
                sheet.close()

```

I haven't done any further testing (and am not likely to have to time to anytime soon, sadly), but it looks like the data object passed ends up being only a dict if there are multiple sheets, with each value in the dict being a list, but if there is only one sheet the list is all that gets passed, rather than a dict with one key and value. If I do get the time I'll test out that theory with a simple multi-sheet file.

issue_title: certbot-auto permissions error
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):

Debian Stretch (although every OS is affected)

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):

certbot-auto

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

```
$ ./certbot-auto                  
Upgrading certbot-auto 0.17.0 to 0.18.0...
Replacing certbot-auto...
Creating virtual environment...
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/virtualenv.py", line 2375, in <module>
    main()
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/virtualenv.py", line 724, in main
    symlink=options.symlink)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/virtualenv.py", line 946, in create_environment
    site_packages=site_packages, clear=clear, symlink=symlink))
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/virtualenv.py", line 1157, in install_python
    mkdir(lib_dir)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/virtualenv.py", line 329, in mkdir
    os.makedirs(path)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 150, in makedirs
    makedirs(head, mode)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 150, in makedirs
    makedirs(head, mode)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 150, in makedirs
    makedirs(head, mode)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 150, in makedirs
    makedirs(head, mode)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/os.py", line 157, in makedirs
    mkdir(name, mode)
OSError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/opt/eff.org'
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

It crashes.

The problem that occurs when upgrading is older versions of `certbot-auto` download a new version of themselves during what's called in the script "Phase 1". After this, the script is rerun with `--le-auto-phase2` and the new version of the script assumes that if you're in "Phase 2", we've already ensured we have root access. This isn't true when "Phase 1" is completed by an older version of the script.

issue_title: WikiText2 dataset incorrect targets
issue_body:
With torchtext v 0.2.1, It seems that the targets returned by the WikiText2 dataset correspond to the next window of `bptt_len` over the dataset. For a language modeling task, shouldn't the targets be simply the next word? 

concretely: `batch.text =  ['<eos>', '=', 'Valkyria', 'Chronicles', 'III']` and `batch.target =  ['=', 'Valkyria', 'Chronicles', 'III', '=']` and it is my understanding that `batch.target = ['=']` would be correct.


full testing code:
```python

from torchtext import data
from torchtext import datasets

TEXT = data.Field()

train_data, val_data, test_data = datasets.WikiText2.splits(TEXT)

TEXT.build_vocab(train_data, min_freq=10)

train_iter, val_iter, test_iter = data.BPTTIterator.splits(
            (train_data, val_data, test_data),
            batch_size=1, bptt_len=5, repeat=False)

batch = next(iter(train_iter))

def recover_text(vector):
    return [TEXT.vocab.itos[int(v)] for v in vector]

recover_text(batch.text.data.squeeze())  # >>> ['<eos>', '=', 'Valkyria', 'Chronicles', 'III']

recover_text(batch.target.data.squeeze()) # >>> ['=', 'Valkyria', 'Chronicles', 'III', '=']
```

Thanks for the awesome package.

-Austin

issue_title: Set JSONField's default to callable
issue_body:
Current implementation is `kwargs.setdefault('default', {})`.
This makes all the model instances share same object reference. So, updating one object's data effects all of the objects and will result in data corruption.
changing to `kwargs.setdefault('default', dict)` will fix the issue.

issue_title: [xray] Sending too much uncommitted lineage when forwarding tasks
issue_body:
### Describe the problem

When forwarding tasks from one raylet to another, the message includes the uncommitted lineage of the forwarded task. However, in many cases, this may include many tasks that have already been sent in a previous task.

In particular, consider a workload where one raylet submits N methods to an actor on a second raylet, and nothing gets evicted yet from the lineage cache. We will end up spending O(N^2) on the `ForwardTask` messages, since each message contains all of the previously submitted actor methods.

We can reduce this to the optimal O(N) if we associate each task entry in the lineage cache with a hash set of the node managers that we've sent it to so far. We can then check this hash set as we walk a task's lineage in `GetUncommittedLineage`. Using this algorithm, each `ForwardTask` message in the workload above should contain only the forwarded task.

### Source code / logs
Adapted from a script provided in #2403. This test starts two raylets and submits 100,000 methods from one actor to another.
```
from __future__ import absolute_import
from __future__ import division
from __future__ import print_function

import ray
import time
import datetime

@ray.remote(resources={"Node1": 1})
class A(object):
    def __init__(self):
        print("Actor A start...")

    def f(self):
        num_of_items = 100 * 1000

        b = B.remote(num_of_items)
        time_str = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S:%f')
        print("push, start " + time_str)
        for i in range(num_of_items):
            b.f.remote(i)
        time_str = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S:%f')
        print("push, end " + time_str)
        time.sleep(5)

@ray.remote(resources={"Node2": 1})
class B(object):
    def __init__(self, num_of_items):
        print("Actor B start...")
        self.num_of_items = num_of_items
        self.sum = 0

    def f(self, object_id):
        #ray.get(object_id)
        #print (object_id)
        self.sum += 1
        if (self.sum == 1) :
            time_str = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S:%f')
            print("read, start " + time_str)

        if (self.sum == self.num_of_items) :
            time_str = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S:%f')
            print("read, end " + time_str)
        if (self.sum % 10000 == 0) :
            time_str = datetime.datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S:%f')
            print("read " + str(self.sum) + "  " + str(time_str))


    def get_sum(self):
        return self.sum


if __name__ == "__main__":
    ray.worker._init(start_ray_local=True, use_raylet=True, num_local_schedulers=2, resources=[
        {
            "Node1": 1,
        },
        {
            "Node2": 1,
        }])
    A.remote().f.remote()
    time.sleep(300)
```

**Durations for the above test**
Legacy ray: 1min
xray: >3min
xray + #2403: >2min
xray + #2403 + artificially limiting the uncommitted lineage to size 1: 45s

issue_title: [webui] Allow timeline scroll-to-zoom without holding ALT
Version mismatches on cluster can lead to silent failures.
issue_body:
To reproduce the problem, take two machines with different cloudpickle versions.

Start Ray on Machine 1.

```
ray start --head --redis-port=6379
```

Start Ray on Machine 2.

```
ray start --redis-address=<machine-1-ip>:6379
```

The second command appears to succeed. However, none of the workers on that machine are alive. Checking `/tmp/raylogs`, the local scheduler log has a lot of errors of the form

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/ubuntu/ray/python/ray/workers/default_worker.py", line 81, in <module>
    ray.worker.connect(info, mode=ray.WORKER_MODE, actor_id=actor_id)

    "    Cloudpickle: " + version_info[3])
Exception: Version mismatch: The cluster was started with:

    Ray location: /home/ubuntu/ray/python/ray/__init__.py
    Python: 3.5.2
    Cloudpickle: 0.5.2
```

It would be simpler to just have the `ray start` command raise that exception.

issue_title: Multicheckboxes HTML IDs breaking change
issue_body:
* Package version: 1.7.1
* Django version: 1.11.11
* Python version: 3.6.4
* Template pack: (Optional) Bootstrap 3

### Description:

I've upgraded to the latest version of django-crispy-forms and our automated QA scripts started failing because it couldn't find the IDs checkboxes in a multi-select widget.

For instance, a field called ages used to generate ids like this `#id_ages_1`, `#id_ages_2`, ... but it's now `#id__1`, `#id__2`... In my case, the field uses `crispy_forms.bootstrap.InlineCheckboxes`.

This seems to be related to:
https://github.com/django-crispy-forms/django-crispy-forms/pull/758

While it's not a problem for a page a single multi-select field, it would cause non-unique IDs if a page were to have more than one field like this.

### Preferably also include:

- [ ] Example Django Crispy Forms code
- [ ] Screenshots
- [ ] Actual HTML generated
- [ ] Expected HTML

I'll see if I can attach a more self-contained example...

issue_title: Windows Installer doesn't add PATH properly?
issue_body:
After installing version 0.2 I tried to start streamlink via a .bat file like this:
`set /p streamname=Stream name:%=%`
`streamlink http://www.twitch.tv/%streamname% best --player-no-close `
but cmd then says it doesn't know the command streamlink.
If I start cmd manually and start a random stream like this:
`streamlink twitch.tv/lirik best`
it works.
If I look at path in the manually started cmd then the entry pointing to the streamlink bin folder is there.
If I add path to the .bat file like this:
`set /p streamname=Stream name:%=%`
`streamlink http://www.twitch.tv/%streamname% best --player-no-close `
`path`
`pause`
then the path to the streamlink bin is missing.
I fixed this by adding `set PATH= path_to_streamlink_bin` as the first line in the .bat but it should work without me adding that.

This issues didn't exist with livestreamer.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'Ticket' object has no attribute 'customer_id'
issue_body:
In our django-helpdesk app, we are allowing non-staff users the ability to update their tickets. However, when attempting to view the ticket (as a non-staff user), the following error is thrown:

`AttributeError: 'Ticket' object has no attribute 'customer_id'`

Looking at the "_is_my_ticket" function in staff.py, if a user is not superuser or staff, then a check is made to verify the user id matches the ticket customer_id. However, the Ticket model does not define "customer_id" as one of its attributes.

Reference: https://github.com/django-helpdesk/django-helpdesk/blob/master/helpdesk/views/staff.py#L92

To get around this, this line has been changed to compare user.email to ticket.submitter_email on our forked repo.

issue_title: [V3] Load locale.po files with the encoding specified in their header
issue_body:
### Type:

- [X] Suggestion
- [ ] Bug

### Description of the problem
Localized files are currently loaded in whatever encoding [locale.getpreferredencoding()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/locale.html#locale.getpreferredencoding) returns (as specified in [open's documentation about encoding](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#open)) which may or may not be the right encoding depending on the platform.

### Potential solution
My suggested solution would be using the encoding specified in the locale.po's header with a fallback on chardet to guess the encoding.

```python
colours = [u'#00ff00', u'#000000', u'#000000', u'#ff0000', u'#ff0000', u'#ffff00', u'#ffff00', u'#00ff00', u'#00ffff', u'#0000ff', u'#0000ff', u'#ff00ff', u'#ff00ff', u'#ffffff', u'#ffffff', u'#00ffff', u'#ff0000', u'#000000', u'#000000', u'#0000ff', u'#0000ff', u'#ff00ff', u'#ff00ff', u'#ff0000', u'#ffff00', u'#00ff00', u'#00ff00', u'#00ffff', u'#00ffff', u'#ffffff', u'#ffffff', u'#ffff00', u'#0000ff', u'#000000', u'#000000', u'#00ff00', u'#00ff00', u'#00ffff', u'#00ffff', u'#0000ff', u'#ff00ff', u'#ff0000', u'#ff0000', u'#ffff00', u'#ffff00', u'#ffffff', u'#ffffff', u'#ff00ff']
```

issue_title: "old" operators do not define shape propagation
issue_body:
As can be seen in e.g. https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/v1.2.1/onnx/defs/math/old.cc the operators do not define the shape propagation function.

This leads to missing results for shape_inference for the model zoo (e.g. VGG19)

This function should be defined even for old operators where semantic for propagation did not change and/or the models shall be updated.

issue_title: High Memory usage of extract_intervals
issue_body:
I am running extract_intervals on an mm10 annotation (from ENCODE but with the transcript_name field added at the end to solve that issue). It has 23337 genes and 30455 transcripts. It seems to take tons of memory - using up all the 64 gigs of ram I have and then eating into swap space, which obviously makes it run very slow - 20’ to process the first 2500 genes. 

How much memory use is expected for the extract_intervals program? I assume there is a problem somewhere here.

issue_title: Support for Drop-seq bam files?
issue_body:
Hi there, 

first of all, thank you very much for the great package and the awesome preprint, really enjoyed reading your analysis using RNA velocity and the theory behind it. 

I was going through the python tutorial and was wondering whether you are planning to add a run mode, that would make it easy to run velocyto with BAM files from the Drop-seq pipeline. 

The format for BAM files created with this pipeline is described here:
[Dropseq Alignment Cookbook](http://mccarrolllab.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/03/Drop-seqAlignmentCookbookv1.2Jan2016.pdf)

Basically, the Cell barcode for each read is tagged with the XC tag and the molecular barcode with the XM tag. I think it would be really cool to use your approach on some Drop-seq data. Maybe there is already a way to use the package on these kinds of BAM files and I haven't noticed if so, please excuse and close the issue. If not, I think there are quite a few people using Drop-seq, that would be interested to apply velocyto to their data! 

Best,

Florian

issue_title: First field can't start with comment prefix even if quoted
issue_body:
Reading the source code, it looks like checking for comment prefixes is done *after* the line has been parsed and un-quoted as a CSV line. This means that a line is interpreted as comment even if the comment prefix is quoted as part of the first field value of the line, which is probably not extended.

(This does not look easy to fix, unfortunately.)

issue_title: How to set outgoing content_type to xml?
issue_body: I'm trying to send some xml to the LinkedIn api, by doing like this: ``` xmlStr = 'This is a comment.This is the titleThis is the descriptionhttp://www.isittuesday.co.ukhttp://stunningplaces.net/wp-content/uploads/2014/05/11-Rio-de-Janeiro-Cochabana-Beach.-Photo-by-ballnkicka.com_.jpgconnections-only' r = linkedInApp.post('people/~/shares', data=xmlStr, content_type='application/xml') ``` But this creates the following request: ``` POST https://api.linkedin.com/v1/people/~/shares?oauth2_access_token=XXXXX Content-Type: application/json;charset=UTF-8 ``` On [this page](http://flask-oauthlib.readthedocs.org/en/latest/client.html) it indeed says that for outgoing requests, OAutlib only supports `'urlencode'` and `'json'`. So I also tried setting the header like this: ``` r = linkedInApp.post('people/~/shares', data=xmlStr, headers={'Content-Type': 'application/xml'}) ``` which gives me the traceback below. Does anybody know how I can get OAuthLib to set the header to `'application/xml'`? All tips are welcome! issue_body:

Can't authenticate with LDAP after moving user to another OU
issue_body:
Hi! 
I have setup LDAP auth in zulip with django_auth_ldap, it work fine, but after moving user to another organization unit (OU) he can't login.
My settings:
```
AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI = "ldap://domain.ru"

AUTH_LDAP_CONNECTION_OPTIONS = {
    ldap.OPT_REFERRALS: 0,
}
AUTH_LDAP_USER_SEARCH = LDAPSearch("ou=MainDepartment,dc=domain,dc=ru",
ldap.SCOPE_SUBTREE, "(&(objectclass=person)(objectclass=user)(mail=%(user)s))")
```

User has been moved from

I don't understand why this makes a problem. Both DN must match the ldap search filter.

issue_title: RDFa TC #117: Fragment identifiers stripped from BASE
issue_body:
vfaronov, 2010-09-20T00:46:18.000Z 
RDFaParser doesn't strip fragment IDs from base URIs, as required by RFC 3986. The enclosed patch fixes this and makes RDFa TC #117 pass.
